name: Tossal Gros de Miramar – circular desde Valls por el Coll de Coloma
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valls offers diverse terrain within Catalonia's picturesque countryside. The region features varied landscapes, including rocky ridgelines, forest singletracks, and areas with significant elevation gains. Trails often traverse natural parks and provide access to technical descents, catering to a range of skill levels. This combination of local routes and access to wider Catalan natural environments makes Valls a base for outdoor adventures. Best mountain bike trails around Valls The most popular mountain bike trail is Tossal Gros de Miramar – circular desde Valls por el Coll de Coloma, a 23.6 miles (38.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes notable climbs and offers expansive views from Tossal Gros de Miramar. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Trail of the Canyon of Death – Torre de la Mixarda loop from Valls, a moderate 22.1 miles (35.5 km) path. This route explores dramatic canyon landscapes and passes by the historic Torre de la Mixarda. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo del Rourell – Pont dels Moros loop from Valls, a 22.4 miles (36.1 km) trail leading through historical landmarks like Castillo del Rourell and Pont dels Moros, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Valls is defined by picturesque countryside, varied terrain, and forest singletracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Valls are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valls's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Valls?
A: Valls offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 80 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Valls?
A: The terrain around Valls is quite diverse, featuring picturesque countryside, varied landscapes, and forest singletracks. You'll encounter areas with significant elevation gains, rocky ridgelines, and technical descents, providing a dynamic riding experience. The region's natural parks also contribute to the varied environments.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Valls?
A: Yes, Valls has options for less experienced riders. There are 8 easy mountain bike routes available, perfect for those looking for a gentler introduction to the region's trails. These routes typically have less demanding climbs and smoother paths.

Q: Where can I find more challenging mountain bike routes near Valls?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Valls offers 10 difficult routes. One such demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38257393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de la Mussara – Pont dels Moros loop from la Plana - Picamoixons&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 60 km and features significant elevation changes, including the climb to Alt de la Mussara.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Valls?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Valls are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14926933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegetal Tunnel of Reeds – Bamboo Tunnel loop from Valls&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 52 km ride through interesting natural features.

Q: What natural features or attractions can I see while mountain biking in Valls?
A: The Valls region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore picturesque countryside and varied landscapes. A notable natural attraction in the wider area is the &lt;a href="https://www.visitavalls.cat/en/discover-valls/nature/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olles de Horta&lt;/a&gt;, natural pools popular for bathing in summer, which provide a tranquil escape. While not directly on every trail, the region's natural allure is a constant backdrop.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks accessible by mountain bike near Valls?
A: Yes, several routes pass by historical points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33850515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Rourell – Pont dels Moros loop from Valls&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1726823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Montblanc&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/193275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/514236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montserrat Sanctuary, Montferri&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3803234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Pere del Puig&lt;/a&gt; on other routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Valls?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valls are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the quality of the routes.

Q: Is Valls a good base for exploring the wider Catalonia region by mountain bike?
A: Absolutely. Valls is well-situated within Catalonia, a region known for its extensive network of mountain biking trails and commitment to preserving its natural heritage. While Valls offers excellent local routes, its location provides access to the broader, diverse natural environments of Catalonia, making it an ideal starting point for wider exploration.

Q: Are there specific mountain passes I can include in my mountain bike routes around Valls?
A: Yes, the Valls area features several notable mountain passes that can be incorporated into your rides for challenging climbs and scenic views. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3310183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Riba–Farena Mountain Road&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Mont-ral&lt;/a&gt;, among others.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valls?
A: Valls, located in Catalonia, generally offers favorable conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide the most comfortable temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are usually mild, allowing for year-round activity, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.
tips: Potable water source located in Prenafeta, population added to the municipality of Montblanc.
Mountain port between Figuerola and Prenafeta with incredible views. From there you can see Tossal Gros (867 meters), the highest mountain in the Miramar mountain range.

La Cogulla of Figuerola del Camp is a mountain of 786 meters that is located between the municipalities of Figuerola del Camp, in the Alt Camp and Montblanc, in the Conca de Barberà. At the top we can find a geodesic vertex.

waypoints.name: La Cogulla de Figuerola del Camp Summit and Miramar Cross
